coverage_note: Paragraph-level seed index. Entries not listed here return 'not_found' (for covered books) or 'unavailable' (for uncovered books). This is an honest statement of coverage, not a claim of absence.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: The serpent's promise 'you will be as God, knowing good and evil' (Gen.3.5) represents the inversion of wisdom: the sensory (external) self claiming it can discern spiritual truth independently of the Lord. 'Eyes being opened' in AC 194 is explained as the opening of the bodily senses to self-derived reason, not the opening of genuine spiritual understanding. The 'knowing' offered here is not yada (experiential encounter-knowledge) but the illusion of self-sufficiency.
entries.note: AC 194-200 covers Gen.3.5 in detail. The serpent corresponds to sensory-level reasoning (sensuale) that inverts the order of influx from the Lord.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: The serpent represents the sensory level of the human (sensuale), the outermost and most external degree of the rational mind. It is 'craftiest of all beasts of the field' because sensory reasoning can construct compelling arguments that appear to support falsehood while seeming rational. AC 195 establishes the serpent as a correspondence for this inverted cognitive state.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 232 interprets 'And Adam knew (yada) Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten (qanah) a man from the LORD.' The internal sense: the celestial man (Adam) conjoining with the affection of truth (Eve) through experiential knowledge (yada). 'Qanah' (gotten/acquired) refers to the acquisition of rational faith from this conjunction. The immediate adjacency of yada and qanah in Gen.4.1 is thus textually structural in Swedenborg's reading of the celestial marriage.
entries.note: AC 232 covers Gen.4.1. The yada–qanah adjacency noted in the lexicon layer aligns with Swedenborg's internal-sense reading of conjugial/celestial knowledge.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: Water in the internal sense signifies knowledges (cognitiones) and truths in general, but also, when corrupted, falsities. AC 680 establishes water as a systematic correspondence throughout scripture: living water = genuine truth from the Lord; floodwater = overwhelming falsities. John 4:14 (living water springing up) and the Flood narrative both employ this correspondence.
entries.note: AC 680 (referenced in dictionary entry for 水). Covers Gen.6.17 context; water correspondence applies broadly.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: Fire in scripture corresponds to love — Divine Love when the fire is holy and life-giving, infernal love (self-love, love of domination) when destructive. AC 934 analyzes the burning bush of Exod.3.2: the fire that burns but does not consume corresponds to Divine Love, which enkindles and purifies without destroying. The angel in the flame = Divine Truth proceeding from Divine Love.
entries.note: AC 934 (referenced in dictionary entry for 火 / Exod.3.2).

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: Light in the Word signifies Divine Truth, and by derivation, intelligence and faith. AC 1053 treats the light associated with the covenant vision (Gen.15.17). The correspondence: the Lord is the Sun in the heavenly world, whose light is Divine Truth illumining the minds of angels and humans. Darkness correspondingly = lack of truth, spiritual ignorance.
entries.note: AC 1053 (dictionary entry for 光 / ヨハネ1:5). The John 1:5 correspondence ('the light shines in the darkness') is thematic extension of this same AC principle.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 3869 addresses 'knowing' (yada) in the context of conjugial love and the celestial marriage of good and truth. In the internal sense, for one spiritual entity to 'know' another is for them to be conjoined in love and wisdom — not merely to possess information about them. This is the basis for reading John 17:3 ('that they may know thee') as eternal life through living union with the Lord, not propositional knowledge about God.
entries.note: AC 3869 is referenced in first_node_yada.json for the yada correspondence. Direct Bible ref in this section is Gen.29.32 context; thematic reach extends to all yada (H3045) occurrences.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 4967 further develops yada as the internal-sense term for the mutual indwelling of good and truth in the celestial degree. 'To know' in this sense is not cognitive acquisition but ontological interpenetration — the kind of knowing that constitutes the very life of heaven.
entries.note: AC 4967 referenced in first_node_yada.json alongside AC 3869.

entries.title: Heaven and Hell
entries.text: HH 118: In heaven, the Lord appears as a Sun. His heat is Divine Love; His light is Divine Truth (Wisdom). Angels nearest the Lord are in the most intense heat and light; those more distant receive graduated influx. The sun of the natural world corresponds to this heavenly Sun: natural warmth/light → spiritual love/truth. Rev.21.23 ('the Lamb is the lamp thereof') is the same correspondence in apocalyptic register.
entries.note: HH 118 (dictionary entry 太陽). The solar correspondence is one of Swedenborg's most foundational and systematic.
entries.source: Heaven and Hell §118 (E. Swedenborg, public domain)

entries.title: Heaven and Hell
entries.text: HH 119: The moon in heaven is seen by those in the spiritual kingdom (as opposed to the celestial kingdom which has the sun). The moon corresponds to faith — truth received intellectually rather than lived out of love directly. As the natural moon reflects the sun's light without being its own source, so faith (faith-from-charity) reflects Divine Truth without being its ultimate source.
entries.source: Heaven and Hell §119 (E. Swedenborg, public domain)

entries.title: Divine Love and Wisdom
entries.text: DLW 1: God is Love Itself and Wisdom Itself — not a being who has love and wisdom as attributes, but who is these as their very being (esse). Love and Wisdom in God are one; the appearance of their distinction is for the sake of human reception. Everything in creation is a receptacle of these two: love as warmth-influx, wisdom as light-influx. 1 John 4:8 ('God is love') is the scriptural anchor for this ontological claim.
entries.note: DLW 1 (dictionary entry 愛). Foundation of the entire correspondence system: all natural warmth/light corresponds to spiritual love/wisdom.
entries.source: Divine Love and Wisdom §1 (E. Swedenborg, public domain)

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 276 treats 'bread' (lechem) as a correspondence for the good of love — the celestial nourishment that sustains spiritual life. Gen.3.19 ('in the sweat of your face you shall eat bread') in the internal sense describes the labor of regeneration: acquiring truth (food/bread) by the effort of the natural man before celestial good is freely given. John 6:35 ('I am the bread of life') is the New Testament pinnacle of this correspondence.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: Trees in scripture correspond to perceptions and knowledges of good and truth. AC 2163 develops this: a tree's roots (hidden, nourishing) = the will; its trunk = the rational; its branches and leaves = natural memory-knowledges; its fruit = goods of life produced therefrom. Matt.7.17 ('a good tree bears good fruit') employs this exact correspondence.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 2088: Sheep in the Word correspond to those who are in the good of charity — specifically the gentle, innocent, and teachable affections of the spiritual person. The shepherd/sheep relation corresponds to the Lord's relationship to those who receive His leading through charity. John 10:11 ('I am the good shepherd') is the explicit New Testament expression of this correspondence.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 343: The shepherd corresponds to the Lord as the one who leads, feeds, and guards those in the good of innocence and charity. One who dwells in tents and tends cattle (Gen.4.20, Jabal) corresponds to the celestial man who tends interior goods. John 10:14 ('I am the good shepherd, I know my own') — the 'knowing' (ginōskō) here is the same relational, mutual indwelling that yada expresses in Hebrew.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 402: A city in scripture corresponds to doctrine — the organized system of truths by which the church is built and governed. Jerusalem = the church as to its doctrine of truth; Babylon = a city whose doctrine is built on self-love and domination. Rev.21.2 ('the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God') = the new doctrine of the New Church descending from Divine Truth.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 870: The dove corresponds to the affection of truth in the spiritual person — especially truth in its gentle, peaceful, and productive aspect. Noah sending the dove (Gen.8.8) = the spiritual person testing whether truth can find a 'resting place' (i.e., can be received into good). Matt.3.16 ('the Spirit of God descending like a dove') = the Divine Truth of the Holy Spirit in its most peaceful and pure influx.

entries.title: Arcana Coelestia
entries.text: AC 878: The hand in scripture corresponds to power and the sphere of operation — what one has the capacity to do and effect. The right hand = the power of goodness/love; the left hand = the power of truth/faith. 'The hand of the LORD' = Divine omnipotence operating through love. Ps.118.16 ('the right hand of the LORD does valiantly') = the power of Divine Good prevailing in regeneration.
